text: During the laparoscopy for total laparoscopic hysterectomy, the surgeon observes that the uterus is densely adherent to the intestines, requiring careful dissection to separate the structures.

The surgeon performs laparoscopic adhesiolysis, carefully dissecting the intestines away from the uterus. The left pararectal space is entered, and the gut is visibly separated from the uterine tissue.
The surgeon opens and drains the right ovarian cysts, then carefully releases the cysts from dense adhesions to the pelvic side wall and intestines.
The surgeon releases the sigmoid colon, which is densely adherent to the uterus and the right uterosacral ligament, carefully separating these structures.

The surgeon dissects and ligates the right uterine artery at its origin from the internal iliac artery, followed by ligation of the left uterine artery at its origin, effectively devascularizing the uterus in preparation for hysterectomy.
The surgeon dissects the sigmoid colon away from the uterus, entering the right pararectal space to completely release the colon from its dense adhesions to the uterine tissue.
The surgeon coagulates and cuts the left round ligament, followed by the dissection of the left ureter. Subsequently, the left infundibulopelvic ligament is coagulated and cut.
The surgeon identifies the right ureter and coagulates and cuts the right infundibulopelvic ligament, separating it from surrounding structures.
The surgeon coagulates and cuts the right round ligament. The peritoneum overlying the bladder is incised, and the bladder is carefully separated from the cervix.
The surgeon isolates, coagulates, and cuts the ascending branch of the right uterine artery. Subsequently, the vaginal vault is opened anteriorly using a co-cup for guidance.

A circumferential incision is made, completely detaching the uterus from the cervix. The uterus is then removed vaginally. The surgeon closes the vaginal vault laparoscopically using a continuous suture technique.
The final view of the pelvis shows the completed surgical field after the hysterectomy, with all ligaments and vessels coagulated and cut, and the vaginal vault closed.
